title: artemisia cana subsp. bolanderi
intro: Artemisia cana subsp. bolanderi is a subspecies of the sagebrush plant, specifically a type of shrubby shrub that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which can be attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Some of the health benefits associated with Artemisia cana subsp. bolanderi include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and calm respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
